title: Fix incidents failing to load (frontend-to-backend connection)

requirements.text: Diagnose and fix the frontend-to-backend connection so that incidents load successfully on both the Dashboard and Admin Panel. The 'Failed to load incidents / Unable to connect to the backend' error must not appear. Audit the full data path: verify the canister ID environment variable is correctly referenced and available at runtime, confirm the useActor hook produces a valid actor instance with anonymous identity for public queries, confirm the useQueries hooks (especially getAllIncidents) receive a non-null actor and invoke backend calls correctly, and ensure no module initialization order or provider wrapping issue (e.g., from ThemeProvider, QueryClientProvider, or InternetIdentityProvider ordering in App.tsx or main.tsx) is silently preventing actor creation. Fix all identified root causes so incidents are fetched and displayed correctly on page load.

quotes: The incidents still fail to load. Fix this.

acceptanceCriteria: The Dashboard loads and displays incidents from the backend without showing any error message.
The Admin Panel loads incident data from the backend without showing any error message.
The useActor hook returns a valid actor instance for anonymous (public) queries.
The getAllIncidents React Query hook successfully calls the backend and returns incident data.
No console errors related to canister ID, actor initialization, or backend connection appear on page load.
The light/dark mode ThemeToggle continues to function correctly alongside the working backend connection.
Internet Identity authentication on the /admin route continues to function correctly.

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths: useInternetIdentity.ts, useActor.ts, main.tsx, and frontend/src/components/ui.
Do not change the backend Motoko actor or data model.
The light/dark mode toggle (ThemeToggle, useTheme) must remain functional after the fix.
Internet Identity authentication on /admin must remain functional after the fix.
nonGoals: Adding new features or UI changes unrelated to fixing the backend connection.
Changing the backend Motoko actor logic.
Modifying the authentication flow beyond what is needed to restore the connection.
